name: Combat
description: Combat skills are used during combat to make use of weapons or to understand the combat situation.

skills.name: Artillery
skills.description: This skill involves the technical training and specialized knowledge required for a character to operate, aim, and maintain heavy weaponry, such as siege engines or large caliber fixed guns.

skills.name: Melee Combat [Weapon Category]
skills.description: This skill represents a character's training and proficiency in fighting in close quarters using any handheld weapon (e.g., swords, clubs, or axes) to physically engage an opponent.

skills.name: Ranged Combat [Weapon Category]
skills.description: This skill measures a character's proficiency in using projectile weapons like bows, slings, or firearms to strike targets at a distance.

skills.name: Tactics
skills.description: This skill is the understanding of battlefield command, small-unit movement, and defensive positioning that a character uses during a dynamic combat encounter.

skills.name: Tracking
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to follow and interpret signs, footprints, and subtle disturbances left by a person, animal, or vehicle across various terrains.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: This skill is a character's practical know-how for living off the land, including finding food, water, and shelter in wilderness environments, and preparing for harsh weather.

skills.name: Observation
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to notice subtle details in their environment, detect traps, find hidden objects, or visually perceive small, fleeting changes.

skills.name: Orientation
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to navigate using natural landmarks, maps, or celestial bodies, ensuring the character does not get lost and can consistently maintain their direction.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: This skill involves a character's training in tumbling, balancing, mid-air maneuvers, and complex body movements to avoid falls or pass through difficult spaces gracefully.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.description: This skill is a character's general physical fitness, stamina, and application of force for activities like jumping, sprinting, long-distance running, or resisting physical strain.

skills.name: Climbing
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to ascend vertical or near-vertical surfaces with speed and competence, whether using specialized gear, ropes, or bare hands and feet.

skills.name: Swimming
skills.description: This skill is a character's technique and stamina required to move efficiently through water, whether for speed, silence, or staying afloat in turbulent or dangerous conditions.

skills.name: Insight
skills.description: This skill is a character's capacity to accurately assess a person's motives, honesty, or mental state by observing their subtle body language and speech patterns.

skills.name: Persuasion
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to rationally argue, charm, negotiate, or inspire others using compelling logic and strong rhetoric to peacefully sway their opinion.

skills.name: Deception
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to knowingly mislead others through false statements, elaborate lies, or fabricated evidence, while maintaining a convincing facade.

skills.name: Intimidation
skills.description: This skill is a character's use of explicit threats, displays of force, or dominant presence to frighten or coerce others into carrying out an action.

skills.name: Performance
skills.description: This skill is a character's talent for entertaining an audience through singing, playing an instrument, dancing, acting, or public speaking.

skills.name: Investigation
skills.description: This skill is a character's focused, rational process of gathering clues, searching crime scenes, and connecting disparate facts to solve a case or logical puzzle.

skills.name: Language
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to speak, read, and write multiple distinct languages beyond their native tongue, allowing for wide communication.

skills.name: Engineering
skills.description: This skill is a character's technical application of scientific principles to the design, construction, and maintenance of structures, vehicles, or complex systems.

skills.name: Science
skills.description: This skill is a character's general theoretical expertise across scientific fields like chemistry, biology, or physics, used for laboratory work, experimentation, or analyzing natural phenomena.

skills.name: Strategy
skills.description: This skill is a character's long-term planning and decision-making skill used for managing large organizations, economies, or conducting political or military campaigns over extended periods.

skills.name: History
skills.description: This skill is a character's comprehensive, specialized knowledge of past events, cultures, eras, and important figures in the world or setting.

skills.name: Ciphers
skills.description: This skill involves the creation, analysis, and comprehensive breaking of secret codes, cryptography, and complex symbolic or encoded languages by a character.

skills.name: Driving
skills.description: This skill is a character's reflexes and coordination needed to control high-speed vehicles, maneuver in tight spaces, or perform vehicular stunts while maintaining control.

skills.name: Demolition
skills.description: This skill is a character's precise application of knowledge (informed by Science skills) used to place and safely detonate explosives to breach structures or destroy targets with accuracy.

skills.name: Mechanics
skills.description: This skill is a character's practical ability to fix, disassemble, modify, and maintain complex mechanical devices, engines, or clockwork systems.

skills.name: Forgery
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to skillfully replicate documents, handwriting, art, signatures, or official seals to create convincing fakes.

skills.name: Electronics
skills.description: This skill is a character's delicate skill of dealing with complex, small-scale electronic devices, sensors, microchips, and digital systems.

skills.name: Electrics
skills.description: This skill is a character's practical knowledge of installing, diagnosing, maintaining, and safely repairing electrical wiring, power grids, and basic circuits.

skills.name: Craft
skills.description: This skill is a character's practical ability to create, forge, weave, or produce items in a specific, hands-on trade like metalworking, tailoring, or ceramics.

skills.name: Trapping
skills.description: This skill is a character's specialized ability of setting snares, pits, and complex mechanical traps to capture animals or deter intruders.

skills.name: Handiwork
skills.description: This skill is a character's general fine motor skills and attention to detail used for small, non-specialized tasks like tying complex knots, performing delicate assembly, or minor repair work.

skills.name: Torture
skills.description: This skill is a character's disciplined, systematic use of physical pain or psychological manipulation to extract information from a captive.

skills.name: Animal Handling
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to work with, calm, train, and command animals through understanding their behavior and establishing trust or dominance.

skills.name: Transmission
skills.description: This skill is a character's technical knowledge of operating and understanding communications equipment, radio frequencies, signal encoding, and long-distance messaging systems.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to move silently, avoid breaking branches, and generally prevent creating noise while moving or taking action.

skills.name: Hiding
skills.description: This skill is a character's capability to find optimal concealment and remain still, utilizing shadows or terrain to avoid being seen or noticed by others.

skills.name: Sleight of Hand
skills.description: This skill is the delicate process of performing close-up illusions, palming small objects, picking pockets, or manipulating tiny components without being detected.

skills.name: Escape Artist
skills.description: This skill is a character's training in contorting their body and exploiting weaknesses to quickly and silently escape from bonds, restraints, manacles, or confined spaces.

skills.name: Disguise
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to convincingly alter their physical appearance, clothing, and mannerisms to pass as another person or blend into a specific crowd.

skills.name: First Aid
skills.description: This skill is the practical application of immediate medical knowledge used by a character to administer emergency care, stabilize a wounded person, or stop severe bleeding.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.description: This skill is a character's advanced knowledge of diagnosis, non-invasive treatment, and management of diseases, injuries, and illnesses, including the effective use of pharmaceutical or herbal remedies.

skills.name: Surgery
skills.description: This skill involves a character's specialized ability to perform delicate, invasive procedures on a patient using precision tools to repair severe internal damage or remove foreign objects.

skills.name: Psychology
skills.description: This skill is a character's ability to understand and assess human behavior, motivation, and emotional states to predict reactions, influence opinions, or provide general counsel.

skills.name: Psychiatry
skills.description: This skill is the study and practice of diagnosing and treating severe mental illnesses, deep trauma, or emotional disturbances that a character uses through structured intervention and therapy.
